rows.name: Hall Effect/Rapid Trigger
rows.note: Keychron's V6 Ultra HE opens Kickstarter July 22 with hybrid Nova Socket TMR+mechanical PCB; PC Gamer and others now call HE/rapid trigger baseline for 2026 gaming boards.

rows.name: HMX Cloud V2
rows.note: No fresh coverage this week; HMX Cloud V2 discussion holds steady as a budget hot-swap linear pick per KeebFinder listings, momentum essentially plateaued.

rows.name: Gateron Oil King
rows.note: Oil King catalog (linear, silent linear, silent tactile, heavy tactile) stays steady in vendor listings; no new SKUs or reviews surfaced this week per gateron.com.

rows.name: Gateron Lanes
rows.note: ThereminGoat posted a new Gateron Lanes switch review on Patreon this month, reviving tactile-switch chatter after the initial spring launch buzz cooled.

rows.name: Cherry MX2A
rows.note: MKBGuide's 2026 Cherry MX guide notes virtually every new Cherry switch now ships as MX2A, repositioning Cherry against Gateron even as ThereminGoat's dummy-switch review keeps hobbyist skepticism alive.

rows.name: GMK CYL OG Extensions
rows.note: The Geekhack interest check keeps climbing as its announced July 15-Aug 12 group-buy window opens, still outscoring both closed GMK CYL buys' peak reads with no vendor listing yet.

rows.name: GMK CYL TA Neo
rows.note: GMK CYL TA Neo group buy closed July 17, 2026 after a June 19 open per KeebFinder tracking; final-week pledges pushed interest to a close-of-buy peak.

rows.name: DCS Handarbeit
rows.note: DCS Handarbeit's group buy wrapped July 19, 2026 (opened June 19); community chatter shifted to delivery-estimate discussion for its Q4 2026 ship window.

rows.name: Split/Ergo layout
rows.note: MKBGuide and Engadget both flag 2025-2026 as the moment split layouts went mainstream, citing Keychron Q13 Max and Dygma as reference boards for the shift.

rows.name: Alice layout
rows.note: Ergonomic buyers increasingly skip Alice as a stepping stone and jump straight to full split, per MKBGuide's split-keyboard guide, continuing Alice's slow fade in enthusiast threads.

rows.name: 75% Layout
rows.note: 75% remains the default custom form factor in vendor catalogs with no major new releases this week; interest holding at a steady plateau.

rows.name: Work Louder x OpenAI Codex Micro
rows.note: OpenAI's Codex Micro macro pad, built on Work Louder's Creator Micro platform, fully launched July 15, 2026 at $230 per TechCrunch and Engadget coverage of OpenAI's first hardware.

rows.name: Mode Designs
rows.note: Mode Designs posted a Summer 2026 update covering Sonnet/Encore shipping status, Prologue 98% progress, and an Envoy final-stock announcement, per green-keys.info.

rows.name: Prototypist
rows.note: Prototypist's Paper 65 kit group buy (opened March 31) continues toward Q4 2026 delivery while its GMK CYL Midnight Rainbow R2 project and 2026 relaunch push keep the vendor visible.

rows.name: Keychron
rows.note: Keychron opened $5 deposits for the V6 Ultra HE ahead of a July 22 Kickstarter, the first board mixing mechanical and TMR magnetic switches via its new Nova Socket PCB, per Notebookcheck and TechPowerUp.

rows.name: Cherry (GMK parent)
rows.note: Cherry's brand score keeps climbing (-24 to -18) while the MX2A switch line falls further behind (-46) — the widening gap this piece tracks: XTRFY's TMR pivot, not the mainline switch business, is what's pulling Cherry's aggregate score off its floor.

rows.name: Wuque Studio
rows.note: Wuque Studio stays quiet this week; its NAMA group buy (closed December 2025, delivery est. March 2026) has cycled out of active discussion per CannonKeys listings.
